programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Is a powerfully rich cabal with a globalist agenda conspiring to destroy sovereign nations and impose authoritarian rule?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Bigfoot
programme.desc: Two prominent Bigfoot trackers and a cryptozoologist affirm the existence of this shy and elusive creature and relate several close encounters with Sasquatch.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Freemasons
programme.desc: Are the Freemasons a harmless fraternity whose members busy themselves by raising money for children's hospitals or a satanic cult whose tentacles and enslave mankind?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The O'Hare UFO Incident
programme.desc: Richard investigates a UFO sighting over Chicago's O'Hare airport that was witnessed by dozens of people, including an airline captain, his crew and several airport officials.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: RFID Chips
programme.desc: Could implantable RFID chips or Radio-frequency identification tags currently in widespread use in retail industry be used to surreptitiously track and control people?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Mokele Mbembe
programme.desc: Mokele Mbembe means able to stem the flow of rivers. The Pygmies there say they have encountered just such a creature and according to their descriptions, this behemoth is a dead ringer for an Apotosaurus.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard speaks with several independent researchers about the efforts of international bankers to control the course of history and enslave humanity through their monopoly of the creation of money and the manipulation of the money supply.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Bilderbergs
programme.desc: A glimpse backstage at the highly secretive annual gathering of the world's richest and most powerful movers and shakers.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: A serious investigation of the long-held theory that Elvis Presley faked his death in August 1977. Jokes of Elvis sightings in Kalamazoo aside, there is a considerable volume of evidence to suggest Elvis may not have left the building

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Ouija Boards?
programme.desc: Harmless board game or portal to the spirit world? Richard delves into the history and folklore of 'talking boards' with a collector of Ouija boards and a world renowned paranormal investigator.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: An investigation into the practice of past-life regression therapy; a technique where subjects are placed under hypnosis in order to recover memories of supposed past lives.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Depopulation Agenda
programme.desc: Richard interviews an investigative health reporter and a NY Times best-selling author about their claims that a powerful cabal is waging war on mankind through forced sterilization, vaccination and other means.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Shroud of Turin
programme.desc: Is this famous piece of linen cloth the actual burial cloth of Jesus Christ or a clever Medieval hoax?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Remote Viewing
programme.desc: We meet several individuals who claim they have the ability to transcend time and space and identify people, objects, and locations hidden from their view.

programme.title: The Why Files: Atlantis World War Pole Shift / The Secret Government Airline That Doesn't Exist
programme.desc: AJ investigates Edgar Cayce, a famous psychic. / AJ investigates the government's secret airline.
programme.sub-title: Atlantis World War Pole Shift / The Secret Government Airline That Doesn't Exist

programme.title: The Why Files: Alien Moon Base: The Incredible Story of Ingo Swann
programme.desc: AJ investigates Ingo Swann, a psychic who implored remote viewing to examine the dark side of the moon.
programme.sub-title: Alien Moon Base: The Incredible Story of Ingo Swann

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: End times prophecy experts discuss the possible identity of the antichrist and the timing of his emergence onto the world state.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Are we really running out of oil, or does the government and Big Oil just want us to think we are? Two proponents of the abiotic oil theory argue that oil is not a fossil fuel and actually replenishes itself deep within the earth.
programme.sub-title: Are We Running Out of Oil?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard meets members of the 9/11 Truth movement who challenge the official version of events concerning the attack on the U.S. Pentagon. Researchers present evidence to suggest the attack was perpetrated by some rogue element inside the U.S. government.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Two victims of alleged electronic harassment and organized gang-stalking recount their horrible ordeals. Richard investigates possible theories as to who is behind this program and why.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: An investigation into the various conspiracy theories revolving around the tragic death of Diana, The People's Princess, in Paris in August 1997.
programme.sub-title: The Death of Princess Diana - Tragic Car Accident or Murder Most Foul?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Area 51
programme.desc: What's going on inside Area 51, America's most carefully guarded secret? The mysterious military base located 30 minutes north west of Las Vegas has been rumored to be involved in back engineering advanced alien technology recovered from UFO crash sites.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Is The Cure for Cancer Being Suppressed?
programme.desc: Richard investigates claims that the medical profession, drug companies, the food industry and government agencies are suppressing a cure for cancer.
programme.sub-title: Is The Cure for Cancer Being Suppressed?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Phoenix Lights
programme.desc: On March 13th, 1997, an extraordinary event occurred across the state of Arizona that has become the subject of great debate in the UFO community.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard interviews the expedition organizer who is planning on leading a team of scientists, filmmakers and tourists to the Arctic aboard a Russian nuclear ice-breaker in search of an entrance into the interior of our world.
programme.sub-title: Journey to the Center of the Earth

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: A lawyer and from the Pacific Northwest claims to have been part of a secret government time travel experiment, known as Project Pegasus.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Did Jim Morrison Fake His Death?
programme.desc: Is it conceivable that the Doors legendary front man did not die in a bathtub in Paris of an apparent drug overdose?
programme.sub-title: Did Jim Morrison Fake His Death?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Rise of the Fourth Reich (Nazi's in America)
programme.desc: The Allies defeated the Axis Powers on the battlefield, but did they actually win the war? Richard interviews several alternative historians who say that while the German Army may have surrendered in 1945, the Nazi's never officially did.
programme.sub-title: The Rise of the Fourth Reich (Nazi's in America)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Execution of MLK and The Framing of James Earl Ray
programme.desc: In this re-examination of the assassination of civil rights leader Martin Luther King, Richard meets several researchers who provide evidence that the convicted gunman, James Earl Ray was not the real triggerman.
programme.sub-title: The Execution of MLK and The Framing of James Earl Ray

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard examines the growing UFO Disclosure movement and its attempts to force the US Government to reveal to the public what it knows about UFOs and aliens.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: GMO - Genetically Modified Food
programme.desc: Richard investigates claims crop plants created for human or animal consumption using the latest molecular biology techniques are unsafe.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard speaks with the pre-eminent investigators of the alleged crash of a UFO near Roswell, New Mexico in 1947.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Could vampires be real? A well-known U.S. paranormal investigator and folklorist in New York City recounts the legend and evolution of the vampire throughout history and from culture to culture.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: JFK
programme.desc: Richard meets with some prominent JFK assassination researchers to discuss the November 1963 murder of President John F. Kennedy.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Water Engine
programme.desc: Did the late Stanley Meyer figure out a way to run an internal combustion engine on pure water? And was he murdered because of the threat his invention posed to Big Oil?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: The legend of the werewolf throughout history and in various cultures is explored. The Conspiracy Show travels to England and rural Wisconsin to investigate claims of actual werewolf sightings.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Philadelphia Experiment
programme.desc: Richard investigates a legendary naval military experiment said to have been carried out at the Philadelphia Naval Shipyard in October 1943.

programme.title: The Remarkable 20th Century: The 1920's
programme.desc: In the 1920's, following the "War to End All Wars," countries that were already amid reforming their semblances of peacetime, found themselves confronted with the need to reinvent and solidify decidedly solidified cultural identities.

programme.title: The Remarkable 20th Century: The 1930's
programme.desc: Following a seemingly worldwide regression, a decidedly active collective effort initiated to establish and balance the increasingly unpredictable nature of modernity.

programme.title: The Remarkable 20th Century: The 1940's
programme.desc: A decade intrinsically linked to the infamous iconography that defined it, the 1940's have become almost synonymous with all things WWII and Holocaust-related.

programme.title: The Remarkable 20th Century: The 1950's
programme.desc: With cultures and world identities solidifying themselves identifiers later recognized as staples of the times beginning to form, the 1950's became a widely identifiable starting point of what we now colloquially refer to as pop culture.

programme.title: The Remarkable 20th Century: The 1960's
programme.desc: The 1960's served as a pivotal transition period, filled with revolutions ranging from social to governmental.

programme.title: The Remarkable 20th Century: The 1970's
programme.desc: With tensions mounting over America's imperial presence in all corners of the planet, combined with juxtaposing dissatisfactions over domestic policy.

programme.title: The Remarkable 20th Century: The 1980's
programme.desc: The 1980's were a time of technological progress, which naturally implemented changes ranging from oppressive regimes to improvements in daily life.

programme.title: The Remarkable 20th Century: The 1990's
programme.desc: With the turn of a new century breathing down its neck, the 1990's were recognizable by the inevitable presence of a new millenium breathing down its neck.

programme.title: The Remarkable 20th Century: The 1900's
programme.desc: After the turn of a century marked by mass innovations released more rapidly than any time period prior, the world's populous was anxious and impatient for what the next could bring.

programme.title: The Remarkable 20th Century: The 1910's
programme.desc: A decade significantly defined by what was then the most deathly and international war in human history, equivalent progressions were demanded by the times in parallel fields, such as art, philosophy and science.

programme.title: The Remarkable 20th Century: The 1930's
programme.desc: Following a seemingly worldwide regression, a decidedly active collective effort initiated to establish and balance the increasingly unpredictable nature of modernity.

programme.title: Secrets of Britain: Secrets of The Tower of London
programme.desc: Standing guard over the city of London for nearly 1,000 years, the formidable Tower of London has been a royal castle, a prison, a place of execution and torture, an armory and the Royal Mint. Now unlock the doors to visit the secret rooms.
programme.sub-title: Secrets of The Tower of London

programme.title: Secrets of Britain: Secrets of Selfridges
programme.desc: Two words are synonymous with Selfridges: luxury and London. However, the store was the brainchild of an American, Harry Gordon Selfridge, who completely revolutionized the way Londoners shopped, turning it into a luxurious adventure.

programme.title: Secrets of Britain: Secrets of Scotland Yard
programme.desc: No police institution in the world captures the public imagination like Scotland Yard. From the world's most famous serial killer to the biggest jewelry heist in history, see how Scotland Yard has sown the seeds of modern-day policing.

programme.title: Secrets of Britain: Secrets of Underground London
programme.desc: On the surface, London is a buzzing, modern metropolis—but underneath lies a hidden world. Uncover 2000 years of subterranean history, including a world of perfectly preserved Roman remains, gruesome plague pits and top secret bunkers.

programme.title: Secrets of Britain: Secrets of Her Majesty's Secret Service
programme.desc: Her Majesty's Secret Service is the world's oldest and most famous spy agency. Witness the truth behind the covert operations, the tales of treachery and the double-agents who've risked their lives.
programme.sub-title: Secrets of Her Majesty's Secret Service

programme.title: Pop Profiles: No Doubt
programme.desc: This episode is dedicated to one of the most iconic pop stars of our time: No Doubt. No Doubt's story is told chronologically through their major hit singles. New interviews add insight into the life of No Doubt.

programme.title: Pop Profiles: Mumford & Sons
programme.desc: This episode is dedicated to one of the most iconic pop stars of our time: Mumford & Sons. Mumford & Sons' story is told chronologically through their major hit singles. New interviews add insight into the life of Mumford & Sons.

programme.title: The Big Interview: Ringo Starr
programme.desc: In the 100th episode of The Big Interview with Dan Rather, two legends sit down together as Ringo Starr discusses what it was like as a Beatle, and the struggles and successes of his life since.

programme.title: Final 24
programme.desc: Featuring archival footage, dramatic reenactments, and interviews with his family and friends, Final 24 documents the final moments of Tupac Shakur’s life.

programme.title: Freddie Mercury: The King of Queen
programme.desc: Renowned as one of the greatest performers of the 20th century Freddie Mercury pushed the boundaries of art and music as he lead his band Queen to international stardom.

programme.title: The Big Interview: Gene Simmons
programme.desc: He's the most famous member of KISS but Gene Simmons is also a tongue wagging, fire spitting media & marketing mogul.

programme.title: Final 24
programme.desc: Final 24 traces the roots of Janis Joplin's addiction and uses archive footage, dramatic reenactments, and interviews with her closest companions

programme.title: The Big Interview: Roger Waters
programme.desc: Rock and roll legend and one of the founders of Pink Floyd, Roger Waters talks with Dan about his music, his politics and his upcoming tour.

programme.title: Lil Wayne: Bling
programme.desc: Releasing his first solo studio album at just the age of 15, Lil Wayne has shotthrough the ranks of the hip-hop scene. He has challenged societal ideas aboutimage and has really become the representative of modern hip-hop. Follow the story of the man...

programme.title: Elvis the Early Years: Part 1
programme.desc: The KING OF ROCK N’ ROLL, from his working class life in Memphis, Tennessee to worldwide super-stardom. Sit at the edge of your seat when Elvis struggles to make his great come-back and enjoy all the unforgettable hits.

programme.title: Elvis the Early Years: Part 2
programme.desc: The KING OF ROCK N’ ROLL, from his working class life in Memphis, Tennessee to worldwide super-stardom. Sit at the edge of your seat when Elvis struggles to make his great come-back and enjoy all the unforgettable hits.

programme.title: Dolly Parton: Queen of Country
programme.desc: Dolly Parton is the most honoured female country performer of all time. She has had 25 songs reach No1 on the Billboard Country chats, a record for a female artists. She has 41 career top 10 country albums, a record for any artist.

programme.title: The Big Interview: Willie Nelson
programme.desc: He's on the road again. Dan catches up with music legend Willie Nelson aboard his new tour bus.

programme.title: Pop Profiles: Michael Jackson
programme.desc: This episode is dedicated to one of the most iconic pop stars of our time: Michael Jackson. Michael's story is told chronologically through his major hit singles. New interviews add insight into the life of Michael Jackson.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star: Depeche Mode
programme.desc: Legendary DJ Rusty Egan and broadcaster Robert Elms discuss the music videos of Depeche Mode and the special relationship between the band and director Anton Corbijn.

programme.title: Jimi Hendrix: The Uncut Story: 1961-1967
programme.desc: As a sideman playing behind some of the biggest names in R&B, Jimi Hendrix started to make a name for himself in music. Not long after, the Jimi Hendrix Experience emerged as the hottest rock trio in New York and across the pond in swinging London.

programme.title: Thin Lizzy: Rocker on the Loose
programme.desc: Phil Lynott was Ireland's first rock and roll international superstar. His hard man image helped him stand out to the world as one of the most influential rockers of the 1970’s.

programme.title: Past Hunters: Tutbury Castle
programme.desc: The team head to Tutbury Castle in Derbyshire to investigate the paranormal reports at the location. Past Hunters appear to have caught the Tutbury Ghost on camera.

programme.title: Past Hunters: Mill Street Barracks
programme.desc: Built in 1861 Mill Street Barracks is steeped in rich history. It has accommodated many branches of the armed forces throughout its life. Like many other barracks it has also been used as a morgue making it a prime location for the supernatural.

programme.title: Past Hunters: Plas Teg
programme.desc: The county of Flintshire in Wales is said to be a land filled with ghosts and haunts. Plas Teg is said to be one of the "most haunted" houses in the area. With over 400 years of history, this place is sure to be the home of many an entity.

programme.title: Past Hunters: Blackpool Pleasure Beach
programme.desc: Blackpool Pleasure Beach is one of the most visited attractions in the United Kingdom. People visit it not only for the fun, but to also hopefully catch a glimpse of the many ghosts that haunt there.

programme.title: Are You Haunted Special: Tutbury Castle Special
programme.desc: Filmed over two nights, this special episode sees the AYH investigating historical Tutbury Castle. The team are joined by their very special guest Lesley Smith, historian, and castle custodian. And Dale has a rather emotional encounter.

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: "A very strange place"
programme.desc: The Are You Haunted team investigates a venue with some amazing history and some bizarre incidents. Everything from unexplained footsteps to apparitions has been witnessed here; and for two members of the crew, it will be a venue they won't forget.

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: A different time
programme.desc: In this episode, the AYH team visit a building that delivers much more than initially meets the eye. Poltergeist activity, unexplained noises and vocal sounds are all experienced...but at which venue?

programme.title: Psychic Investigators: Forced Entry
programme.desc: Stacy Hendrickson is a college student living in Tempe Arizona. Away from home for the first time, she vanishes one night from her apartment.

programme.title: Psychic Investigators: Finding Amy
programme.desc: In Portland Maine, a young woman named Amy St. Laurent goes out one evening with friends. At a bar she meets a stranger named Jeff.

programme.title: Psychic Investigators: Decoy Dolly
programme.desc: Late one evening in suburban Louisiana, a young carpenter offers a lift home to a women he’s met in a bar. But after riding off with the stranger in his truck, Andre disappears into the night.

programme.title: Psychic Investigators: River Rat
programme.desc: An elderly widow is brutally murdered in her small town Louisiana home.

programme.title: Psychic Investigators: Tunnel Vision
programme.desc: Two teenage girls are killed horribly, months apart, on the outskirts of the northern Canadian city of Thunder Bay.

programme.title: Psychic Investigators: How Dark the Woods

programme.title: Psychic Investigators: Home After Dark
programme.desc: Seventeen year-old Tyson Efird disappears from the parking lot of the grocery store where he works in Malvern, Arkansas.

programme.desc: Happy couple Billy and Billie Jean are found shot and stabbed in their home, with a baby still cradled in Billie Jean’s arms. The investigation reveals the reason might be found in a bizarrely bitter online feud.

programme.title: In Plain Sight: Deck the Halls with Murder
programme.desc: Hard working celebrity small businesswoman Mary Ann Clibbery is found bludgeoned inside her own remodelling business just before Christmas, and police are completely stymied as to who would want this well liked entrepreneur dead.

programme.title: In Plain Sight: Murder in the Cabin
programme.desc: Multi-millionaire Bob Bosley is gunned down in his remote cabin, and his wife Amy claims a robber killed Bob and dashed off into the woods. A wide search of the woods turns up nothing, but a search of Bob’s lifestyle turns up many interesting details.

programme.title: In Plain Sight: Vicious Vixen
programme.desc: Soon-to-be mother Cindy Thompson is brutally murdered in a horrifying night-time attack. While Cindy and her boyfriend Mark are excited for the arrival of this new baby, police eventually discover that not everyone felt the same way.

programme.title: In Plain Sight: Murder for a Price
programme.desc: When the comfortably wealthy couple Dennis and Merna Koula are violently gunned down in their suburban home, police suspect a home invasion. But then police start to look for someone who thinks they would be more profitable dead than alive.

programme.title: In Plain Sight: Death in Marriage
programme.desc: Carolyn Stahl is expecting a surprise for her birthday, but instead ends up being gunned down alongside her husband in their car. It looks like a professional hit, but what police can’t tell is who the intended target is?

programme.title: In Plain Sight: Killer Payback
programme.desc: Gary Triano has lost a fortune through bad investments, and somebody wants to get their money back. But who wants it bad enough to blow him up?

programme.title: In Plain Sight: Kill Pill
programme.desc: When Sue Snow takes some pills in the morning and suddenly dies from cyanide poisoning, police think they may have another random “Chicago Tylenol Poisoning” on their hands. But then a second victim appears.

programme.title: In Plain Sight: Journal of Death
programme.desc: Recently separated single mother Dana Laskowski is found strangled to death in her home on a night when her triplets are visiting their father. The crime scene suggests that she knew her attacker.

programme.title: In Plain Sight: Star-Crossed Killer
programme.desc: After mother-to-be Rhoni Reuter is shot to death, at first all attention is directed at the baby’s father, former NFL football player Shaun Gayle. However, it is soon discovered that Shaun has many more girlfriends than just Rhoni.

programme.title: In Plain Sight: Blood on the Ranch
programme.desc: When 55year old jogger Bob Dorotick is found dead by the side of a countryside road with tire tracks leading from the scene, it is obvious he was murdered. But what is mysterious is that he wasn’t hit by a car, he was bludgeoned, drugged and strangled.

programme.title: In Plain Sight: Killer Swingers
programme.desc: When thirty-eight year-old Robert Limon is shot while working in a remote train yard, video surveillance reveals both a limping man and a motorcycle heading to the crime scene. Which one was the real killer?

programme.title: In Plain Sight: Friends with Killer Benefits
programme.desc: When Derrick Dorsey finds his wife Kim dead beside their bed, and he tells 9-1-1 that he thinks it’s a suicide. Police find it’s a strange conclusion, considering there are bullet holes in the wall and zip ties around Kim’s wrists.

programme.title: In Plain Sight: Secrets Can Kill
programme.desc: When police informant Susan Smith disappears without a trace, police just assume she has just taken off again. But her sister Shelby keeps insisting that Susan’s mystery boyfriend was up to no good it starts to look like she’s right.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: Scenario for a Murder
programme.desc: Since October 10th 2008, no-one has seen friendly 38 year-old bachelor John Altinger. All that his friends know is that he was going to a garage in a residential area of Edmonton, to meet a woman he’d met on an online dating site.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: Mother's Intuition
programme.desc: 14-year-old Pamela Bischoff misses her curfew on April 12, 1991. Though she has only been missing a few hours, her mother, Gail, tells the Oromocto RCMP she is certain Pamela has been raped and murdered by an older boy named Billy Stillman.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Tornadoes
programme.desc: George embarks on his annual pilgrimage to the "Tornado Alley" area of the central U.S. to guide groups of people who want to witness the brute force of tornadoes in action

programme.title: Angry Planet: Island Caving
programme.desc: Accompanied by a group of hardcore cavers from Alberta and British Columbia, George descends into spectacular caves that lead deep into the earth on Vancouver Island. The team carries inflatable kayaks to explore a dark underground river.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Polynesian Volcanoes
programme.desc: In New Zealand, George learns that an undersea volcano 1000 miles North in Tonga is creating a new island. After nautical mishaps, clad in nothing but underwear and a life jacket, George becomes one of the first explorers on this new land.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Desert Monsoon
programme.desc: Enlisting the help of fellow storm chaser R.J. Evans, George heads to Arizona to photograph spectacular lightning storms triggered by the desert monsoon season. R.J. however is known as "America's noisiest athiest" for his free-thought radio show.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Thunder Down Under
programme.desc: In Australia George teams up with storm chaser Jimmy Deguara to witness summer storms in the outback. While in Oz, he dives the magnificent Great Barrier Reef, and explores just how painful is the sting of the venomous Box Jellyfish.

programme.title: Naked Science: How To Avert an Asteroid Impact
programme.desc: Throughout history, the earth has been bombarded from space. Asteroid impacts have caused mass extinctions – including the dinosaur wipe out.
programme.sub-title: How To Avert an Asteroid Impact

programme.title: Naked Science: Making Mountains
programme.desc: The Rockies. Magnificent and awe-inspiring mysterious, the Rockies are one of the longest mountain ranges in the world. Naked Science investigates the geological processes that build these massive mountains.

programme.title: Naked Science: Anatomy Of Tornado
programme.desc: Tornados are one of the most destructive forces of the planets with winds at 300 miles an hour. They can destroy buildings and hurl cars hundreds of feet through the air.

programme.title: Naked Science: The Great Flood
programme.desc: Almost every culture on Earth tells the story of an ancient flood – details vary but the basic plot is the same - An angry God wipes out an unrepentant population.

programme.title: Naked Science: What's Wrong With Pluto
programme.desc: Naked Science investigates the violent cosmological forces that create planets and discover what it is that makes a planet a planet.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Wildfires
programme.desc: With flames roaring to heights of 50 meters or more and temperatures exceeding 1,200°C, the wildfire is one of Mother Nature’s most horrific killers. Wildfires show no signs of stopping and their inexorability on this planet is truly terrifying.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Blizzards
programme.desc: With ice, freezing temperatures, and ferocious winds — the Blizzard is one of Mother Nature’s most effective killing machines. They are typically associated with heavy snow, but may even occur when there is little or no snowfall.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Floods
programme.desc: Even when below your knees, you can still be swept off your feet by the torrent of water. And the danger doesn’t stop there: electric shocks, contaminated water and washed away vehicles are perilous.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Pandemics
programme.desc: With an ever more mobile world population, potentially growing in size to 11 billion people by the next century, public health experts agree that the possibility of a cataclysmic disease is more likely now than ever before.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Hurricanes
programme.desc: With a combination of torrential rain, powerful winds, and devastating storm surges, Hurricanes are among Mother Nature’s most calamitous natural disasters.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Winds Of Autumn
programme.desc: This year he starts off by getting prepared in a wind tunnel, then he and his chase partner Mark Robinson head to Newfoundland for more wild weather action. Then, it's off to another encounter where the wild wind fans a raging inferno.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Iceland: Fire and Ice
programme.desc: George meets President of Iceland Ólafur Grímsson who spearheaded use of geothermal power; visits Blue Lagoon and geysers; explores volcanically active Haimaey Island and climbs an iceberg calving off the glaciers at Vatnajokull Lagoon.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Avalanches
programme.desc: Whenever there is unstable snow and gravity, gravity always wins. George travels to the Rocky Mountains to learn about how to stay alive in the back country.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Venezuela Lightning
programme.desc: Gas from Catatumbo swamps mixed with cold air from the Andes create a perpetual lightning storm on South America's largest lake. From here George canoes 84 km to world's highest waterfall, swims with piranhas and holds a huge Anaconda.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Arctic Winter
programme.desc: George links up with two experienced polar adventurers to explore frigid Canadian Arctic winter. The team uses skis to cross the fractured sea ice of Frobisher Bay, then dog teams to head North through the spectacular Auyuittuq Mountains.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Midwest Stormfest
programme.desc: This season the tornadoes begin early - in February. Through the season, George follows the big weather from Missouri west to the Dakotas, and dodges the huge, destructive Greensburg tornado in southern Kansas.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Wild Water
programme.desc: In this episode, George investigates the wild side of water in dangerous class 5 river rapids, a flooded cave, and even in the ocean surrounded by massive Great White Sharks.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Blizzards
programme.desc: Winter weather can be even more dangerous than thunderstorms and where else to explore the big chill than at the summit of Mount Washington which has the reputation as being the place with the "Worst weather in the world".

programme.title: Angry Planet: Defying Gravity
programme.desc: George experiences extremes of aviation and space training aboard Czech-built Albatross fighter jet, inside Zero-G plane, simulating zero gravity by diving 30,000 feet in giant parabolas and in spinning centrifuges producing 6 G's of force.

programme.title: Naked Science: How To Destroy A Planet
programme.desc: The Earth is built to last. It is a 4,550,000,000-year-old, 5,973,600,000,000,000,000,000-tonne ball of rock and iron. It has taken more devastating asteroid hits in its lifetime than you've had hot dinners, and it still orbits happily around the sun.

programme.title: Africa River Wild
programme.desc: On the Luangwa River, the law of the land is dictated by the changing seasons and the constant cycle of life. In the wet season, the Luangwa Valley is a green paradise. The river’s waters flow in abundance and lush vegetation carpets the landscape.

programme.title: Africa River Wild
programme.desc: The Limpopo River flows along a 1000 mile course through South Africa and Mozambique and out into the Indian Ocean. Four months of the year, this powerful river continuously revitalizes the landscape nourishing the abundance of wildlife dependent on it.

programme.title: Africa River Wild
programme.desc: The Hoanib River runs for around 170 miles. It rises in the Hoanib Valley in Namibia and flows through the parched Namib Desert all the way to the Skeleton Coast before emptying out into the Atlantic Ocean.

programme.title: Africa River Wild
programme.desc: In Northern Botswana lies the magnificent Okavango Delta one of the most extraordinary ecosystems in Africa. In the heart of the Kalahari Desert, it’s a wetland surrounded by parched and arid plains.

programme.title: Africa River Wild
programme.desc: The Sand River is home to one of the densest and diverse animal populations in Africa, where territories dangerously intertwine and overlap. Animal mothers struggle to try and raise offspring to adulthood without them being killed.

programme.title: Africa River Wild
programme.desc: The Zambezi River rises from a marsh in Zambia. It then flows east carving a path across six different African countries before it drains out into the Indian Ocean. The river creates one world in the wet season and another in the dry season.

programme.title: Africa River Wild
programme.desc: The Nile River, Africa’s largest, longest and most ancient of rivers. Spanning 4000 miles the Nile flows through 11 countries before finally emptying out into the Mediterranean. For millennia its waters have sustained life and civilization.

programme.title: Africa River Wild
programme.desc: On the Luangwa River, the law of the land is dictated by the changing seasons and the constant cycle of life. In the wet season, the Luangwa Valley is a green paradise. The river’s waters flow in abundance and lush vegetation carpets the landscape.

programme.title: Africa River Wild
programme.desc: The Limpopo River flows along a 1000 mile course through South Africa and Mozambique and out into the Indian Ocean. Four months of the year, this powerful river continuously revitalizes the landscape nourishing the abundance of wildlife dependent on it.

programme.title: Africa River Wild
programme.desc: The Hoanib River runs for around 170 miles. It rises in the Hoanib Valley in Namibia and flows through the parched Namib Desert all the way to the Skeleton Coast before emptying out into the Atlantic Ocean.

programme.title: Africa River Wild
programme.desc: In Northern Botswana lies the magnificent Okavango Delta one of the most extraordinary ecosystems in Africa. In the heart of the Kalahari Desert, it’s a wetland surrounded by parched and arid plains.

programme.title: Africa River Wild
programme.desc: The Sand River is home to one of the densest and diverse animal populations in Africa, where territories dangerously intertwine and overlap. Animal mothers struggle to try and raise offspring to adulthood without them being killed.

programme.title: Luke Gamble's Vet Adventures: Zambia
programme.desc: Luke goes to stop the illegal practice of selling puppies on the streets. Helps an abused baboon, a pack of lions, finds the sex of infant cats and tests monkeys for tuberculosis. He investigates the death of buffaloes, and visits an elephant orphanage.

programme.title: Luke Gamble's Vet Adventures: Costa Rica
programme.desc: Luke is outside his comfort zone as he travels the Central American rainforests helping a host of different animals. Luke must pass a local surgical exam before he is allowed to operate on castaway dogs.

programme.title: Luke Gamble's Vet Adventures: Thailand
programme.desc: Luke aids a dog shelter that is desperate for veterinary help, visits a hill tribe village where he meets a monk who rescues chickens used for fighting, and helps elephants marred by land mines.

programme.title: Luke Gamble's Vet Adventures: Malawi
programme.desc: Luke visits a country with hardly any vets where he faces the challenges of saving a starved dog with a new litter of pups, anesthetizing a leopard, and tracking in the bush, where he meets a troop of over familiar baboons!

programme.title: Luke Gamble's Vet Adventures: Nepal
programme.desc: Luke goes to work helping neglected equine and battling an STD blighting many street dogs. After a visit with an injured steppe eagle, he heads up the mountain to help an isolated community, where he gets his hands on some hairy yaks.

programme.title: Luke Gamble's Vet Adventures: Grenada
programme.desc: Luke visits the Caribbean adventures take him island hopping to help wild and domestic animals, including a sea turtle caught in a fisherman's net. One of his patients requires an unusual operation and Luke discovers a new use for buttons!

programme.title: Graveyard Carz: "I'm a Ragtop Man!"
programme.desc: Mark assists Justin in buttoning up the rear end details of the 1970 roadrunner convertible.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz: Elephant In A '69
programme.desc: A new season of Graveyard Carz, the show wherein abandoned and left­for­death classic Mopars are given a new lease on life.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz: Family Ties
programme.desc: Two 1969 Hemi Charger R/T 4-speed cars arrive at the shop one still runs so Mark and Doug go for a spin!

programme.title: Graveyard Carz: This is a Crack Up!
programme.desc: The team takes on the biggest engine challenge of their lives! A numbers matching 383 for a 1971 'Cuda that has multiple massive freeze cracks in the block. Mark puts a local shop to the test as they attempt to save an engine.

programme.title: Car Hunters: Rural Rider
programme.desc: Suzi Fife is a busy mom who's looking for a cool sports utility vehicle to remind her of her childhood days on a farm. Along the way, she checks out a '78 International Harvester Scout II with vintage appeal, a souped up '70 Ford Bronco with a 3-speed.

programme.title: Car Hunters: Chip Off the Old Big Block
programme.desc: Growing up around his father's hotrods, Clint Hrabal always dreamed of owning one of his own. His search puts him behind the wheel of a classic '34 Ford Pickup hotrod with a huge engine.
programme.sub-title: Chip Off the Old Big Block

programme.title: Car Hunters: Muscle Mission
programme.desc: Rick Blough is an army veteran who wants to fulfill his lifelong dream of owning a classic car. On his hunt, he test drives a '57 Chevy Bel Air upgraded with the latest parts and technology, a '66 Ford Mustang with a smokin' hot red paint job.

programme.title: Car Hunters: Fastbacks and the Furious
programme.desc: Dallas Choat from Albion Nebraska is on the hunt for a new ride to cruise around town in style. Faced with this tough decision, he puts the pedal to the metal on three high-octane test drives. First, Dallas burns rubber in '67 Mustang Fastback.

programme.title: Car Hunters: Burning Rubber
programme.desc: Sonny Recla is a Dallas firefighter with a true love for classic cars, and he's looking for a new ride to cruise around town in. On his hunt, he test drives a beautiful but compact '55 Ford Thunderbird, a '75 Oldsmobile Hurst with plenty of power.

programme.title: Car Hunters: Fast Money
programme.desc: Jimmy Kent has been in love with cars since he was a teenager, and now he's in the market for a classic sports car that's also be a smart investment.

programme.title: Car Hunters: Drop Top and Roll
programme.desc: Former correctional officer Mark Piland wants to kick of his retirement in style by checking something off his bucket list: owning a classic convertible. First up, a '66 Pontiac LeMans with a powerful V8 engine; then a '71 Mercury Cougar.

programme.title: Car Hunters: Buick Bounty
programme.desc: Tim Sweely is a die-hard Buick fanatic who has finally saved up enough to buy one of his own. His hunt for his perfect Buick puts him in the driver's seat of a luxurious '59 Buick Super, a sleek convertible '65 Buick Wildcat.

programme.title: Car Hunters: Style for Miles
programme.desc: Kelly Huff is an LA based music producer who's on the hunt for a 50s classic that's as original as he is. Along the way he finds an immaculate '57 Ford Thunderbird that drives like a dream, a '56 Mercury Monterey that takes him straight down memory lane.

programme.title: Car Hunters: Big Block Bonding
programme.desc: Jim Bussell and his son Chance have a real need for speed. While on the hunt for a car they can bond over, they check out in a rare '72 Barracuda that might take a chomp out of Jim's wallet, a 1970 Nova with surprisingly modern features.

programme.title: Car Hunters: Roads Of Glory
programme.desc: John Dishong is an empty nester who wants to recapture his youth with a car from his past. First up, a '74 Dodge Dart hits the bulls eye on price; then a '70 Buick Electra sparks a jolt of nostalgia...

programme.title: Car Hunters: Highs and Lows
programme.desc: Luis Mendoza has worked on other peoples' low-riders for years, but he's never owned one himself. However, that's all about to change, if he can decide what to spend his hard earned cash on.

programme.title: Car Hunters: Motor City Memories
programme.desc: Jim Welch fell in love with classic cars growing up in Detroit, and he's finally ready to buy one of his own. On his hunt, Jim zips down the road in a '71 Plymouth Road Runner, flies by in a '69 Pontiac Firebird 400.

programme.title: Car Hunters: Investment Ignition
programme.desc: Ron and his wife Angie are looking for a fun convertible that's also a smart investment. On their hunt, they test drive a zippy '59 Porsche 356 replica, a smoking-hot '58 Chevy Corvette, and a numbers-matching '66 Corvette Sting Ray.

programme.title: Car Hunters: Couple's Cruiser
programme.desc: Donald and Cora Durbin are busy parents to four young kids, so they're looking for a classic ride to make their rare date nights extra special. On their hunt they check out a unique '63 Studebaker GT Hawk, a classic '48 Chrysler Windsor convertible.

programme.title: Car Hunters: Generation Restoration
programme.desc: Growing up, Aaron Leider spent quality time working on cars with his dad. Now he and his own son are on the hunt for a car they can restore together. On their search they check out a '69 Lincoln Continental Mark III with a surprise up its sleeve.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz: Mark Unwrapped
programme.desc: A new season of Graveyard Carz, the show wherein abandoned and left­for­death classic Mopars are given a new lease on life.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz: A Fly in the Ointment
programme.desc: A new season of Graveyard Carz, the show wherein abandoned and left­for­death classic Mopars are given a new lease on life.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz: Roaming the Restorations
programme.desc: A new season of Graveyard Carz, the show wherein abandoned and left­for­death classic Mopars are given a new lease on life.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz: Is That a Freckle?
programme.desc: Fresh from the flight and still exhausted from the SEMA show in Las Vegas Mark and the Ghoulz get back to the grind. Will eagerly prepares to paint his very first 1970 AAR 'Cuda.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz: Orange You Glad It's K2?
programme.desc: The Ghouls team up and knuckle down to power through an epic SEMA build! A tribute to a 1970 Dodge T/A Challenger in a color that Will has never painted: Go Mango! AKA Vitamin C for all the Plymouth fans.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz: To Beep or Not to Beep
programme.desc: While the ghouls finish up the ornamentation and chrome moldings for the 1969 GTX Cousin Dougie enlists the Greenhorn's help to disassemble our first 1970 Road Runner - with careful precision to hopefully find the car's broadcast sheet.
programme.sub-title: To Beep or Not to Beep

programme.title: Graveyard Carz: The 'Cuda has a Challenger
programme.desc: After years of lockdown the Ghoulz are back at SEMA! But first they need to finish the tribute 1970 Dodge T/A Challenger. This gorgeous custom build is outfitted in the Graveyard's inimitable OEM style.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz: 67 Hemi GTX Convertible Resurrected
programme.desc: A new season of Graveyard Carz, the show wherein abandoned and left­for­death classic Mopars are given a new lease on life.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: A Man For Elly
programme.desc: Granny loves one TV Western star, Quirt Manly, and Granny already has him set up for Elly May, because she knows he is probably the only who can tame the vine-swinging girl.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: The Giant Jackrabbit
programme.desc: A kangaroo invades the world of the Clampetts, but Granny thinks it’s a big rabbit.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: The Girl From Home
programme.desc: An old beau shows up to marry Jethro for his money.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Lafe Lingers On
programme.desc: Lafe Crick returns to the Clampett mansion looking to get rich off of Jed's money. He tries to get a job at the bank guarding Jed's money, only to get a few million of it.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: The Race For Queen
programme.desc: Bob Cummings plays himself and must judge a beauty contest.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: The Clampetts Strike Oil
programme.desc: In the first episode Jed Clampett strikes oil, sells his land and moves to a 35 room mansion in Beverly Hills.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Getting Settled
programme.desc: When they arrive at their new mansion they are mistaken for backwoods servants.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Meanwhile, Back At The Cabin
programme.desc: The Clampetts find that the comforts of the mansion do not always compare with the comforts of their shack back home.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: The Clampetts Meet Mrs Drysdale
programme.desc: When Mr. Drysdale describes Mrs. Drysdale as being a hypochondriac, the Clampetts assume she must be a drunk.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Jed Buys Stock
programme.desc: When Drysdale suggest buying good stock, Jed quickly buys some cows, pigs and chickens. Meanwhile, Granny has plans to sober up Mrs. Drysdale, who the family believes is a lush.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Trick Or Treat
programme.desc: The Clampetts are feeling homesick so they decide to go visit their new neighbours. Little do they know, it is Halloween, a holiday they have never heard of.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: The Servants
programme.desc: Drysdale attempts another step insocial renovation of the Clampetts by lending them his servants.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Jethro Goes To School
programme.desc: A private school teacher is shocked to learn that her new fifth grade student is Jethro.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Elly's First Date
programme.desc: Elly May is set up with Sonny Drysdale. When they meet, the date ends very soon, after Sonny tries to kiss Elly’s hand, but she thinks he is trying to bite her.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Pygmalion And Elly
programme.desc: Sonny Drysdale plays Pygmalion and Julius Caesar as he resumes his courtship of Elly May.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Elly Races Jethrine
programme.desc: Will Sonny propose to Elly? Can Pearl marry her daughter off? Utter confusion ensues.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: The Great Feud
programme.desc: Sonny jilts Elly and the Clampetts load their shootin’ irons for a match with the Drysdales.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Home For Christmas
programme.desc: When Granny, Uncle Jed, Elly May and Jethro decide to return to the country for the holidays, they discover airline travel.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: No Place Like Home
programme.desc: Home for Christmas, the Clampetts help Pearl woo Mr. Brewster, a big oil company executive.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Jed Rescues Pearl
programme.desc: Pearl attempts to snag Mr. Brewster until he makes a ridiculous public announcement.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Back To Californy
programme.desc: Jed is confronted with too many cooks when he invites Pearl and Jethrine to Beverly Hills.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Jed's Dilemma
programme.desc: Jed takes the family on a sight seeing tour of Beverly Hills to cool a feud between Granny and Pearl.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Jed Saves Drysdale's Marriage
programme.desc: Drysdale’s marriage is threatened when he gets a new housekeeper while his wife is away.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Elly's Animals
programme.desc: Police and Elly’s animals converge on the Clampett estate when Pearl starts giving yodeling lessons.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Jed Throws A Wing Ding
programme.desc: Two of Pearl’s ardent suitors come to visit her in Beverly Hills.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Jed Plays Solomon
programme.desc: Granny’s plan to stop Pearl’s yodeling backfires when the police come and find her still.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Duke Steals A Wife
programme.desc: Duke, Jed’s bloodhound, becomes matchmaker for his master and Mlle. Denise, a glamorous Frenchwoman.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Jed Buys The Freeway
programme.desc: A confidence man tries to sell Jed the Hollywood Bowl and a Freeway.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Jed Becomes A Banker
programme.desc: Jed is made a bank vice president so he can compete in an interbank skeet shoot.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: The Family Tree
programme.desc: An expert on American history finds evidence that Jed’s family preceded the Mayflower to America.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Jed Cuts The Family Tree
programme.desc: Mrs. Smith Standish is spending more time with the Clampetts, desperate to find out if the Clampetts did in fact precede the ‘Mayflower.’

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Granny's Spring Tonic
programme.desc: Granny hands around the Spring Tonic to the family, giving Jed a double dose. At the bank, Gloria transforms herself into a gorgeous secretary, with her sights set on Jed.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies: Jed Pays His Income Tax
programme.desc: Granny thinks a man from the IRS is a revenuer and chases him off with her gun.

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Project Mercury Advances - America's first manned space program finally puts a man in orbit. Camera Tracking - The array of special cameras that follow a spacecraft at launch. The International Space Station - The building and operation.
programme.sub-title: Project Mercury Advancement, Camera Tracking & The International Space Station

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: The Shuttle Program - The first re-useable spacecraft. The Challenger Disaster - Pressing launch schedules lead to carelessness and the loss of the Challenger. Return to Space - A re-designed shuttle is back in space with a redefined role.
programme.sub-title: The Shuttle Program, The Challenger Disaster & Return to Space

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Beyond Earth Orbit - The Apollo program takes bold new steps. IXV Re-entry Vehicle - Europe is working to perfect a re-entry vehicle. Galileo Satellites Lost and Found - Navigation satellites go into incorrect orbit.
programme.sub-title: Beyond Earth Orbit, IXV Vehicles & Galileo Satellites Lost and Found

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Project Gemini Advances - NASA refines the techniques needed for rendezvous. Cassini at Saturn - In the Cassini probe's final years of activity, its handlers are asking it to do more complex and risky assignments.
programme.sub-title: Project Gemini Advances, Cassini at Saturn & More About Mars

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: The Space Shuttle Matures - The Space Transportation System changes the nature of orbital missions. Complex Construction in Space - Confidence in the shuttle begins to return and engineers for get the lessons of the past.
programme.sub-title: The Space Shuttle Matures, Complex Space Construction & The Final Years of the Space Shuttle

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Rebuilding Project Apollo - After the Apollo 1 disaster NASA resumes manned space flight. Orbiting Carbon Observatory 2 - Monitoring the Earth's Co2 levels in unprecedented detail. Zero Gravity Training - Parabolic flights.
programme.sub-title: Rebuilding Apollo, Orbiting Carbon & Zero Gravity Training

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: The Beginning of the End - After the first Moon landing the Apollo science program was about to start in earnest but people were losing interest. Apollo Gets Complicated - New technology is added that extends the reach of the Apollo program.
programme.sub-title: The Beginning of the End, Apollo Gets Complicated & The End of Apollo

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: To the Moon - The Apollo program achieves its major objective. Dragon, Falcon and Spacex - New directions in re-usable spacecraft. Gaia- Mapping our galaxy in unprecedented detail.
programme.sub-title: To The Moon, SpaceX's Dragon and Falcon & GAIA

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Join Brian Greene on a wild ride into the weird realm of quantum physics, which governs the universe on the tiniest of scales.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: Universe or Multiverse?
programme.desc: Cutting-edge theories are suggesting that our universe may not be the only universe. Instead, it may be just one of an infinite number of worlds that make up the multiverse.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: What Is Space?

programme.desc: The latest updates on the new space hardware about to launch us into the next generation of space flight some rockets and capsules are on time others not so fortunate.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Gravity
programme.desc: LISA pathfinder, has led the way for a space based gravity wave detector, what are gravity waves and why are they so hard to detect?

programme.title: The New Frontier: From There To Here
programme.desc: Series introduction and the study of Earth from orbit.

programme.desc: As scientists and astronomers peer closer at nearby stars with ever more fidelity they are discovering planets; exo planets, worlds outside our own solar system.

programme.desc: Interviews with Apollo 8 astronauts, their wives, mission control staff, and journalists give true insider's perspective to the space race of the late 1960's.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Tickets Please

programme.desc: When video-game enthusiast, Sarah is transported into her favorite game, she finds herself on an intergalactic prison, home to the most dangerous villains in the galaxy.

programme.title: Wildest Indochina: Malaysia – Freaks of Nature

programme.title: Wildest Indochina: Vietnam - Phoenix from the Ashes
programme.desc: Vietnam is the easternmost country on the Indochina peninsula in Southeast Asia. Around ten percent of the world’s species live here. But less than 50 years ago, things were very different. Vietnam’s forests were decimated.

programme.title: Wildest Indochina: Thailand – The Wild Heart
programme.desc: Thailand is a land of extremes. A country shaped by wild forces, where animals face tough challenges to survive. Yet they have evolved into some of the most iconic and successful species on the planet.

programme.title: Wildest Indochina: Southwest China - China’s Secret Garden

programme.title: Wildest Indochina: Cambodia – The Water Kingdom

programme.title: Wildest Indonesia
programme.desc: Cloaked in tropical rainforest and warmed by clear blue seas Indonesia is a paradise. Although much of the island chain receives sporadic rain throughout the year, this alone wouldn’t be enough to sustain the region’s rich abundance of life.

programme.title: Wildest Indonesia
programme.desc: Indonesia’s enduring heat provides energy for it’s reptilian residents to thrive, and so much so, they outnumber it’s mammalian inhabitants.

programme.title: Wildest Indonesia
programme.desc: The island chain of Indonesia is home to more active volcanoes than any other country in the world. Yet somehow, instead of posing a threat – this volcanic activity is a source of life

programme.title: Wildest Indonesia

programme.title: Wildest Indonesia
programme.desc: Scattered across the warm South China Sea, Indonesia’s islands are a melting pot for evolution and diversity. Here, rich forests, rivers and coastlines have offered pockets of isolation, enabling animals to develop incredible behaviours and looks.

programme.title: Warship: Life at Sea: Episode 1

programme.title: Warship: Life at Sea
programme.desc: The ship and its crew are heading towards Crimea, when a Russian ship HMS Duncan has been tracking suddenly disappears. As they get closer to Crimea, the situation escalates

programme.title: Warship: Life at Sea: Episode 3

programme.title: Warship: Life at Sea: Episode 4

programme.title: Pompeii's Final Hours: New Evidence
programme.desc: Presenters join a live dig and forensically scan the victims.

programme.title: Pompeii's Final Hours: New Evidence
programme.desc: Presenters look into clues from the day before the eruption.

programme.title: Pompeii's Final Hours: New Evidence
programme.desc: Dormant for 700 years, Vesuvius unleashed its full horror.

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 1
programme.desc: It's plate versus plate as 12 teams-of-two attempt to out-dine and out-wine each other to see whose kitchen rules. Tonight, it is New South Wales' turn as Greek siblings Steve and Helen prepare their three course menu.

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 2
programme.desc: Tonight, we travel to Western Australia to see teacher Angela and truckie Justine prepare their three-course menu. These gals are polar opposites in lifestyle so what will end up on the plate?

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 3
programme.desc: Tonight, it's South Australia's turn as florist Leigh and “princess” Jennifer prepare their Thai inspired three-course menu. These girls are all about style but how will it translate to the plate?

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 4
programme.desc: Tonight, it's the Kiwis turn as we travel to Auckland to see Simon and Meg prepare their three course menu. And it's trans-Tasman war when they put pavlova on the menu.

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 5
programme.desc: Tonight, it's Queensland's turn to cook as solider mates David and Scott host their instant restaurant. The boys are used to high-pressure situations but how will they go under fire in the kitchen?

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 6

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 7
programme.desc: Tonight, it's New South Wales turn to cook as potential in-laws Sam and Jillian prepare their three-course menu.

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 8
programme.desc: Tonight, we head to Perth to see engaged scientists Emma and Andrew host their instant restaurant. Will they have the right formula to win?

programme.title: Kitchen Nightmares: La Parra de Burriana
programme.desc: La Parra de Burriana's menu boasts 72 options, but raw kebabs and slow service threaten to put it out of business. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Kitchen Nightmares
programme.desc: After 30 years and a quadruple heart bypass, Brian still puts in 120 hours per week in the kitchen. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Kitchen Nightmares: Rococo
programme.desc: Nick runs Rococo, an eatery in King’s Lynn. Despite debts of £100,000, Nick continues to cook comatose in a 1990s fantasy land. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Kitchen Nightmares: Morgan's
programme.desc: Chef Phil can’t keep up with the restaurant's menu, Sandy can’t keep the books straight and the restaurant is in debt. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Kitchen Nightmares: La Parra de Burriana Revisited
programme.desc: Gordon returns to La Parra de Burriana to see how the restaurant fares after last season's turnaround. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Kitchen Nightmares: The Fenwick Arms Revisited
programme.desc: Gordon returns to The Fenwick Arms to see how the restaurant fares after last season's turnaround. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Kitchen Nightmares: Ruby Tates
programme.desc: Gordon comes to rescue Ruby Tates, a fancy oyster bar in Brighton that’s burdened by a soft owner and lackadaisical chefs. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Kitchen Nightmares: Piccolo Teatro
programme.desc: Gordon goes to Paris in hopes of helping a petite vegetarian restaurant in the heart of a meat-loving country. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Kitchen Nightmares: The Priory
programme.desc: The Priory boasts a beautiful location and bargain roast dinners. Despite the atmosphere, the food is straight from hell. Contains strong language.

programme.title: MasterChef Australia: Episode 20
programme.desc: On the losing end of a team challenge the day before, the Blue team now find themselves facing off against one another in a three round Thai themed elimination challenge.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Camera Clues
programme.desc: Forensic Photography: Forensic photographers are among the first people at a crime scene, capturing vital clues on film. What do the cameras capture that can't be seen first-hand, and who are the men and women who analyze the camera's clues?

programme.title: The New Detectives: Stranger Than Fiction
programme.desc: A millionaire is found dead, murdered for a stash of buried silver. A young woman dates violent men, only to be killed by her best friend.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Invisible Death
programme.desc: Poison is an almost invisible form of death, and toxicologists must look for hidden clues in blood and tissue to bring these murders to light.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Hidden Obsessions
programme.desc: In San Diego, California, a killer has left behind pieces of evidence. Detectives must sort through these small clues to prove murder.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Grave Secrets
programme.desc: Killers often attempt to deflect attention away from their crimes by hiding the remains of their victims. Bodies may lay hidden for years before they are discovered.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Silent Killers
programme.desc: Weeks pass as forensic investigators search for even the smallest clues, only to find what they feared in their own backyard. In Canada, two people die suddenly of unknown causes, and their deaths may not be as coincidental as they first appear.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Military Justice
programme.desc: The Naval Criminal Investigative Service uses forensic science to solve three perplexing murders and fulfill their motto: To the living we owe respect; to the dead, we owe the truth.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Burning Evidence
programme.desc: Identifying Burned Remains: It's difficult to have a murder investigation without a body, and burning up the victim is a time-honored method of destroying physical evidence.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Without A Trace
programme.desc: Missing Person: Approximately 1.8 million Americans are reported missing each year. Worldwide, the number of missing persons nearly triples.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Killing Time
programme.desc: A killer may strike in the middle of the night, and hide clues well. But the police are always there, ready and working, and they will never give up when they're on the trail of criminals who decide it's Killing Time.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Flames Of Justice
programme.desc: Arson fires are set for their own sake, or to cover a different crime, such as murder. Think fire consumes all vital clues? Think again. Arson investigators can glean important clues from scorched rubble and ignite the unquenchable flames of justice.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Predators And Parasites
programme.desc: For the forensic entomologist, the insects that nest in dead bodies are like tiny witnesses to a crime.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Shattered Vows
programme.desc: A woman is found dead at the bottom of the basement stairs. As detectives look into the accident, they begin to question the sequence of events.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Coroner's Casebook
programme.desc: A good coroner provides what's necessary to solve a crime. A bad one can spoil an otherwise rock-solid case. Cyril Wecht and Henry Lee, two of the country's most respected coroners, share their cases and insights into crime solving.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Face Of Tragedy
programme.desc: Forensic Sculpting: Forensic sculptors retrieve people from oblivion. Using clay and an intricate knowledge of anatomy, forensic arts place a face on an unidentified skull, recreating the victim's likeness, which often leads to his name.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Deadly Target
programme.desc: Ballistics: A corpse is found with a gunshot wound to the head; the weapon lies next to the victim. It looks like suicide, but could it be murder? It's a question best solved by ballistics experts.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Death Grip
programme.desc: Fingerprinting: The Identification Division of the FBI relies on fingerprints as one of the most effective ways to identify criminals.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The contestants visit one of Gordon Ramsay's restaurants; the teams prepare meals inspired by Ramsay's cuisine; the contestants face elimination.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: A team competition tests the contestants' knowledge of pork, with the winners receiving an indoor sky-diving experience; during dinner service, both teams face struggles.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: After both teams are reorganized, the chefs blindly choose ingredients from which they must create a unique dish; the chefs must serve a five-course meal to UNICEF Ambassador Angie Harmon and other VIP attendees.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The red team and the blue team each create their own menus, from which guests can order during dinner service, the team that impresses diners with its food and service wins the challenge, contestants face elimination.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The red team and the blue team each create their own menus, from which guests can order during dinner service, the team that impresses diners with its food and service wins the challenge, contestants face elimination.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The remaining contestants face the blind taste test, with the winning team getting an ice skating lesson with figure skater Rachael Flatt, and the contestants struggle to stay organized during the dinner service.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Ramsay challenges the teams to create entrees with high dollar values, using ingredients purchased on a small budget. Three previous winners judge individual contestants' entrees.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The contestants prepare a dinner service with guests from OxFam America, including Minnie Driver, and two chefs struggle with their stations.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The remaining contestants travel to the Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum, where they cook gourmet versions of tailgate party food favourites for hungry football fans.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: One contestant's overly speedy delivery arouses Gordon's suspicions. Will they be able to win back his respect during the first dinner service of the series?

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: In the second show of the series, the men hope to redress the balance after last week's disastrous start as they take on a beach side challenge. Later, a ruined risotto could spell the end of the line for one unlucky hopeful.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Despite having three chefs ejected, it looks like the blue team's fortunes could be improving after another fraught dinner service. Will raw fish and overcooked meat spell the end for more than one hopeful from the red team?

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Gordon challenges the teams to cater a school graduation ceremony before they face another star-studded dinner service. With certain hopefuls bent on sabotage, which team will emerge victorious?

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: After a protein test challenge, the teams gear up for another stressful dinner service, this time attended by Gordon's mother Helen. One team finishes way in the lead, but which of the hopefuls will be taking the fall this week?

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The teams must herd farm animals into their proper pens and decide which proteins and starches they will use next.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: This time, the teams compete to create the best Italian opera-inspired dishes, with the winning team getting a shopping trip and a VIP dinner. Later, the teams provide dinner service for members of the Italian Consulate.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Fiery chef Gordon Ramsay puts two teams of aspiring chefs through a series of tough challenges. The teams meet new members before heading into a team challenge in which they must prepare a tasting menu for a dog show.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The teams roll the dice to determine the ingredients for their next team challenge, while a contestant leaves due to an injury. The cooks cater for music legends during dinner service, including Stewart Copeland and Steven Tyler.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Following the departure of two contestants from the competition, those remaining are challenged to create Indian-inspired meals, with the winning team earning a day of pampering at a luxurious spa.

programme.title: Andromeda: A Rose in the Ashes
programme.desc: Dylan is imprisoned on a prison planet and must stage an escape as his crew tries to stage their own rescue.

programme.title: Andromeda
programme.desc: Castalians board the Andromeda to finalize the charter for the New Commonwealth. When the leader is discovered dead, Tyr is the main suspect.

programme.title: Andromeda
programme.desc: Beka learns that her father's former business partner deals in a highly addictive drug called F lash.
programme.sub-title: The Pearls That Were His Eyes

programme.title: Andromeda
programme.desc: The Andromeda discovers her sister ship, the Pax Magellanic. The crew aboard Pax is 300 years old, but have not aged a day.

programme.title: Andromeda: Music of a Distant Drum
programme.desc: Tyr crash lands the Eureka Maru on a Nietzschean planet and develops amnesia. He must remember what he is doing before the Nietzscheans discover him and his crate of stolen goods.

programme.title: Andromeda: Harper 2.0
programme.desc: Harper receives the gift of knowledge from a dying Perseid, only to discover that the Perseid was being sought by a bounty hunter who has now turned his attention to Harper.

programme.title: Andromeda: Forced Perspective
programme.desc: Dylan is arrested for assassinating a dictator 300 years ago.

programme.title: Andromeda: Fear and Loathing in the Milky Way

programme.sub-title: Fear and Loathing in the Milky Way

programme.title: Andromeda: The Devil Take the Hindmost
programme.desc: Dylan and Rev Bem help a peaceful society to stand up to their would-be slavers. They have to be careful because if Dylan teaches them to kill, their kids will be born knowing it.

programme.title: Andromeda: The Honey Offering
programme.desc: Dylan attempts to bring peace to two different Nietzschean prides by escorting the princess of one to her arranged marriage to the prince of the other.

programme.title: Andromeda: Star-Crossed
programme.desc: Rommie's avatar falls in love with what turns out to be the avatar of a warship named Gabriel. Gabriel attempts to infect Rommie with a virus that broadcasts its plans to the warship.

programme.title: Andromeda: It Makes a Lovely Light
programme.desc: Beka takes the Andromeda to Tarn-Vedra, but extended time in the slipstream causes side effects. To combat them, Beka starts taking Flash and soon becomes addicted.

programme.title: Andromeda
programme.desc: An old memory from the Andromeda is accidentally activated, which sends her on a mission and leaves Dylan and the crew unable to override the command.

programme.title: Andromeda
programme.desc: Dylan and Rommie must rescue Tyr, Harper and Rev from a Magog Worldship.

programme.title: Andromeda

programme.title: Andromeda

programme.desc: The crew looks for a long-lost Commonwealth leader who has gone into hiding.
